title: memcmp should only be called with pointers to trivially copyable types with no padding
description: memcmp compares the raw memory representation of two objects (what the standard calls their object representation). When objects are not trivially copyable or contain padding, they could have different raw memory representations even though they store identical values. So the result of memcmp is not meaningful.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: memcmp should only be called with pointers to trivially copyable types with no padding.
